pattern: For each node return two totals — the best if you take this node and the best if you skip it — and combine children's totals bottom-up so no two adjacent nodes are ever both taken.
hints: Robbing a node forbids robbing its direct children, so the choice at a node depends on the choices made at its children.
Have each subtree report two numbers: the most it can yield with its root robbed, and the most with its root left alone.
If you rob a node, you add its value to the 'skip' totals of both children; if you skip it, each child contributes the larger of its two totals. The answer is the larger of the root's two numbers.

edge_inputs.description: skip root, rob the deeper layer
rob the two children instead of root plus grandchildren
greedy 'take the root' loses to taking both children

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes, values in range
stress.note: first element non-null so the root exists
